sentences.text: The base machine-level architecture supports only unresumable non-maskable interrupts (UNMIs), where the NMI jumps to a handler in machine mode, overwriting the current `mepc` and `mcause` register values.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0

sentences.text: If the hart had been executing machine-mode code in a trap handler, the previous values in `mepc` and `mcause` would not be recoverable and so execution is not generally resumable.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0

sentences.text: The Smrnmi extension adds support for resumable non-maskable interrupts (RNMIs) to RISC-V.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0

sentences.text: The extension adds four new CSRs (`mnepc`, `mncause`, `mnstatus`, and `mnscratch`) to hold the interrupted state, and one new instruction, MNRET, to resume from the RNMI handler.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0

sentences.text: The `rnmi` interrupt signals are inputs to the hart.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals

sentences.text: These interrupts have higher priority than any other interrupt or exception on the hart and cannot be disabled by software.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals

sentences.text: Specifically, they are not disabled by clearing the `mstatus`.MIE register.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals

sentences.text: The RNMI interrupt trap handler address is implementation-defined.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI Handler Addresses

sentences.text: RNMI also has an associated exception trap handler address, which is implementation defined.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI Handler Addresses

sentences.text: NOTE: For example, some implementations might use the address specified in `mtvec` as the RNMI exception trap handler.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI Handler Addresses

sentences.text: This extension adds additional M-mode CSRs to enable a resumable non-maskable interrupt (RNMI).
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI CSRs

sentences.text: .Resumable NMI scratch register `mnscratch` include::images/bytefield/mnscratch.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI CSRs

sentences.text: The `mnscratch` CSR holds an MXLEN-bit read-write register which enables the RNMI trap handler to save and restore the context that was interrupted.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI CSRs

sentences.text: .Resumable NMI program counter `mnepc`. include::images/bytefield/mnepc.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI CSRs

sentences.text: The `mnepc` CSR is an MXLEN-bit read-write register which on entry to the RNMI trap handler holds the PC of the instruction that took the interrupt.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI CSRs

sentences.text: The low bit of `mnepc` (`mnepc[0]`) is always zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI CSRs

sentences.text: On implementations that support only IALIGN=32, the two low bits (`mnepc[1:0]`) are always zero.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI CSRs

sentences.text: If an implementation allows IALIGN to be either 16 or 32 (by changing CSR `misa`, for example), then, whenever IALIGN=32, bit `mnepc[1]` is masked on reads so that it appears to be 0.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI CSRs

sentences.text: This masking occurs also for the implicit read by the MNRET instruction.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI CSRs

sentences.text: Though masked, `mnepc[1]` remains writable when IALIGN=32.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI CSRs

sentences.text: `mnepc` is a *WARL* register that must be able to hold all valid virtual addresses.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI CSRs

sentences.text: It need not be capable of holding all possible invalid addresses.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI CSRs

sentences.text: Prior to writing `mnepc`, implementations may convert an invalid address into some other invalid address that `mnepc` is capable of holding.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI CSRs

sentences.text: .Resumable NMI cause `mncause`. include::images/bytefield/mncause.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI CSRs

sentences.text: The `mncause` CSR holds the reason for the RNMI.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI CSRs

sentences.text: If the reason is an interrupt, bit MXLEN-1 is set to 1, and the RNMI cause is encoded in the least-significant bits.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI CSRs

sentences.text: If the reason is an interrupt and RNMI causes are not supported, bit MXLEN-1 is set to 1, and zero is written to the least-significant bits.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI CSRs

sentences.text: If the reason is an exception within M-mode that results in a double trap as specified in the Smdbltrp extension, bit MXLEN-1 is set to 0 and the least-significant bits are set to the cause code corresponding to the exception that precipitated the double trap.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI CSRs

sentences.text: .Resumable NMI status register `mnstatus`. include::images/bytefield/mnstatus.edn[]
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI CSRs

sentences.text: The `mnstatus` CSR holds a two-bit field, MNPP, which on entry to the RNMI trap handler holds the privilege mode of the interrupted context, encoded in the same manner as `mstatus`.MPP.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI CSRs

sentences.text: It also holds a one-bit field, MNPV, which on entry to the RNMI trap handler holds the virtualization mode of the interrupted context, encoded in the same manner as `mstatus`.MPV.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI CSRs

sentences.text: If the Zicfilp extension is implemented, `mnstatus` also holds the MNPELP field, which on entry to the RNMI trap handler holds the previous `ELP` state.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI CSRs

sentences.text: When an RNMI trap is taken, MNPELP is set to `ELP` and `ELP` is set to 0.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI CSRs

sentences.text: `mnstatus` also holds the NMIE bit.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI CSRs

sentences.text: When NMIE=1, non-maskable interrupts are enabled.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI CSRs

sentences.text: When NMIE=0, all interrupts are disabled.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI CSRs

sentences.text: When NMIE=0, the hart behaves as though `mstatus`.MPRV were clear, regardless of the current setting of `mstatus`.MPRV.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI CSRs

sentences.text: Upon reset, NMIE contains the value 0.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI CSRs

sentences.text: Software can set NMIE to 1, but attempts to clear NMIE have no effect.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI CSRs

sentences.text: For the purposes of the WFI instruction, NMIE is a global interrupt enable, meaning that the setting of NMIE does not affect the operation of the WFI instruction.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI CSRs

sentences.text: The other bits in `mnstatus` are reserved; software should write zeros and hardware implementations should return zeros.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI CSRs

sentences.text: MNRET is an M-mode-only instruction that uses the values in `mnepc` and `mnstatus` to return to the program counter, privilege mode, and virtualization mode of the interrupted context.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > MNRET Instruction

sentences.text: This instruction also sets `mnstatus`.NMIE.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > MNRET Instruction

sentences.text: If MNRET changes the privilege mode to a mode less privileged than M, it also sets `mstatus`.MPRV to 0.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > MNRET Instruction

sentences.text: If the Zicfilp extension is implemented, then if the new privileged mode is y, MNRET sets `ELP` to the logical AND of yLPE (see ) and `mnstatus`.MNPELP.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > MNRET Instruction

sentences.text: When an RNMI interrupt is detected, the interrupted PC is written to the `mnepc` CSR, the type of RNMI to the `mncause` CSR, and the privilege mode of the interrupted context to the `mnstatus` CSR.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI Operation

sentences.text: The `mnstatus`.NMIE bit is cleared, masking all interrupts.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI Operation

sentences.text: The hart then enters machine-mode and jumps to the RNMI trap handler address.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI Operation

sentences.text: The RNMI handler can resume original execution using the new MNRET instruction, which restores the PC from `mnepc`, the privilege mode from `mnstatus`, and also sets `mnstatus`.NMIE, which re-enables interrupts.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI Operation

sentences.text: If the hart encounters an exception while executing in M-mode with the `mnstatus`.NMIE bit clear, the actions taken are the same as if the exception had occurred while `mnstatus`.NMIE were set, except that the program counter is set to the RNMI exception trap handler address.
sentences.section: Preamble > "Smrnmi" Extension for Resumable Non-Maskable Interrupts, Version 1.0 > RNMI Interrupt Signals > RNMI Operation
